##NAMES=Chinchorroite
##RRUFFID=R250032
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Na_2_Mg_5_(As_2_O_7_)_2_(AsO_3_OH)_2_(H_2_O)_10_
##LOCALITY=Torrecillas mine, Iquique Province, Chile
##OWNER=Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Colourless blades in association with camanchacaite (R250031), espadaite (R250051),  magnesiofluckite (R250052),  picaite (R250053) and ríosecoite (R250054). Type sample described in Kampf et al (2019)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250032

##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
